key_insight: Gravitational instabilities are best viewed as a latent system state that evolves through damage accumulation, stress redistribution, and external forcing until catastrophic failure occurs.
engineering_relevance: This framework helps engineers connect mechanics, monitoring, and forecasting by focusing on reconstructing proximity to failure rather than searching for one-size-fits-all precursors.
method: Treat instability as progressive state evolution
Link internal damage and stress changes with external forcing
Infer hidden system state from incomplete observations
evidence_signal: The abstract presents a conceptual synthesis across geomechanics, fracture mechanics, statistical physics, and data-driven sciences rather than a specific dataset or quantitative evaluation.
watch_item: The framework is conceptual, so practical forecasting still depends on reliable sensing, model calibration, and the ability to infer hidden states from sparse and noisy measurements.
paper_title: A Unified Conceptual Framework for Gravitational Instabilities: From Latent System State to Failure
